entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'No need, All I need is you to have 3 things

A full text editor that is front and back end compatable (pure ASM no monaco maybe OpenGL or DirectX as the base so the frame of the IDE has structure)

Chat pane for front(user) and backend(agent/model)

A file opener for sharing files between front and back end'

Chat Edit: 'PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> cd D:\professional-nasm-ide
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> .\build-dx-ide.bat

========================================
  Building Pure ASM DirectX IDE
========================================

[ERROR] NASM not found in PATH
Please install NASM from https://www.nasm.us/
Press any key to continue . . . 
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> cd bin
Set-Location: Cannot find path 'D:\professional-nasm-ide\bin' because it does not exist.
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> nasm_ide_dx.exe
nasm_ide_dx.exe: The term 'nasm_ide_dx.exe' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program.
Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> # Terminal 1 - Start Ollama server    
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> ollama serve
Error: listen tcp 127.0.0.1:11434: bind: Only one usage of each socket address (protocol/network address/port) is normally permitted.
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> 
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> # Terminal 2 - Build and run your ASM IDE
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> cd D:\professional-nasm-ide
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> .\build-dx-ide.bat

========================================
  Building Pure ASM DirectX IDE
========================================

[ERROR] NASM not found in PATH
Please install NASM from https://www.nasm.us/
Press any key to continue . . .
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> cd bin
Set-Location: Cannot find path 'D:\professional-nasm-ide\bin' because it does not exist.
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> nasm_ide_dx.exe
nasm_ide_dx.exe: The term 'nasm_ide_dx.exe' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program.    
Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
PS D:\professional-nasm-ide> '
